bloc_name: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States)
summary: ECOWAS adopted a Common External Tariff (CET) on October 25, 2013. The CET uses a 5-band tariff structure (0%, 5%, 10%, 20%, 35%) for harmonized tariffs across West African member states.

sources.name: ECOWAS Trade Information System (ECOTIS)
sources.description: Official ECOWAS platform for CET information and trade defense measures
sources.features: Complete CET structure and accompanying trade defense measures

sources.name: tralac - ECOWAS CET Resources
sources.description: Trade Law Centre ECOWAS resources including downloadable CET documentation

sources.name: ECOWAS CET Launch Document
sources.description: Official CET launch and structure documentation (via Nigeria Customs Service)

sources.name: ECOWAS CET Policy Document
sources.description: ECOWAS CET policy framework and implementation details

sources.name: ECOWAS Aid for Trade
sources.description: ECOWAS Aid for Trade programmes related to CET implementation

sources.name: WTO - ECOWAS Review
sources.description: WTO Trade Policy Review of ECOWAS (2022)

sources.name: Research Paper - ECOWAS CET Impact
sources.description: Research analysis of ECOWAS CET tariff lines and structure

sources.name: IGC Blog - ECOWAS CET Analysis
sources.description: Analysis of ECOWAS CET impact on consumers and trade

implementation.note: Individual member states may have additional derogations or special provisions

next_steps: Access ECOTIS portal for official CET master schedule
Download CET PDF from tralac or ECOWAS sources
Extract complete list of HS codes with tariff band assignments
Note any country-specific derogations or exceptions
notes: ECOWAS CET is the primary tariff harmonization mechanism for West Africa. The 5-band structure provides common framework across 15 member states. ECOTIS is the official source for current CET and trade defense information. tralac provides accessible downloadable versions.
